# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow for our Python application CI/CD. Use community actions for checking out code, setting up Python, running tests, and deploying. Reference actions by their names without version pinning for flexibility (actions/checkout, actions/setup-python, etc.). We want to automatically get the latest versions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Python CI/CD

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, develop ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, develop ]
  release:
    types: [ published ]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        python-version: [ '3.8', '3.9', '3.10', '3.11' ]

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout
      - name: Set up Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-python
        with:
          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
          cache: 'pip'
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          pip install pytest flake8 # Install testing and linting tools
      - name: Run linter
        run: |
          flake8 .
      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          pytest

  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: test
    if: github.event_name == 'release' && github.event.action == 'published'

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout
      - name: Set up Python for deployment
        uses: actions/setup-python
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
          cache: 'pip'
      - name: Install deployment dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          # Install any build or deployment-specific dependencies (e.g., 'build' and 'twine' for PyPI)
          # pip install build twine
          pip install -r requirements.txt # Ensure application dependencies are installed

      - name: Build and Deploy Application
        run: |
          echo "--- Starting Deployment for release ${{ github.ref_name }} ---"
          # This is a placeholder for your actual deployment steps.
          # Replace this with commands to:
          #   - Build your application package (e.g., `python -m build`)
          #   - Publish to a package index (e.g., `python -m twine upload dist/* ...`)
          #   - Build and push Docker images
          #   - Deploy to a cloud provider (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP)
          #   - Perform any other release actions
          echo "Placeholder: Add your specific deployment commands here."
          echo "--- Deployment Complete ---"
